headline: KDDI Corporation disclosed a breach of a shared email system that exposed up to 14.2 million logins across six Japanese internet providers.

emerging_trends.text: Breaches keep flowing through shared infrastructure and third-party suppliers, from KDDI's email platform serving five rival carriers to outside vendors holding student records.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Third-Party Breaches Strain the Education Sector

emerging_trends.text: Russian intelligence keeps sharpening credential theft, with fake technical-support texts now reaching the messaging accounts of officials across Ukraine, Europe, and the United States.
Criminals increasingly bend legitimate developer tooling toward fraud, turning the DCloud Uni-App framework into roughly 200,000 investment-scam sites.
emerging_trends.topic_title: DCloud Uni-App Framework Powers 200,000 Scam Sites

topics.title: KDDI Email Breach Hits Six Japanese ISPs
topics.latest_developments: KDDI Corporation disclosed on June 28 that intruders reached an email system it operates for five other Japanese internet service providers, exposing up to 14.2 million email logins.
topics.summary: The compromised platform serves KDDI and five partner carriers across Japan; affected customers should reset their email passwords and watch for credential stuffing against reused logins.

sources.title: Data breach exposes up to 14.2 million email logins at six ISPs

topics.title: Russian Intelligence Steals Messaging Credentials
topics.latest_developments: Ukraine's Security Service said on June 27 that it and the FBI uncovered a long-running Russian intelligence campaign using fake technical-support texts to break into the messaging accounts of government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists across Ukraine, Europe, and the United States.
topics.summary: The operation phishes Signal and other messengers, lately coaxing victims into surrendering their Signal Backup Recovery Key to restore the backup and read private message history; targets should reject unsolicited support messages and guard backup keys.

sources.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
FBI: Russian hackers now target Signal backup recovery keys
FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys

topics.title: Third-Party Breaches Strain the Education Sector
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading reported on June 27 that rising third-party attacks force schools and universities to defend student data against ransomware funneled through their vendors.
topics.summary: Attackers increasingly reach educational institutions through breached suppliers, putting student records at risk; schools and universities need tighter vendor vetting and continuous monitoring.

sources.title: Third-Party Breaches Teach Education Sector a Costly Lesson in Vendor Risk

topics.title: DCloud Uni-App Framework Powers 200,000 Scam Sites
topics.latest_developments: SecurityWeek reported on June 27 that threat actors sell investment-scam templates built with the legitimate DCloud Uni-App toolkit, fueling roughly 200,000 fraudulent sites.
topics.summary: DCloud's Uni-App is a legitimate cross-platform development toolkit; criminals repackage it into ready-made investment-scam templates and sell them, spawning around 200,000 fraudulent sites that bilk victims.

sources.title: Chinese Framework Powers 200,000 Scam Sites

topics.title: Cisco Buys Astrix and WideField for Non-Human Identity
topics.latest_developments: Cisco moved on June 26 to acquire Astrix and WideField, folding non-human identity controls into its security stack and betting that identity becomes the primary control plane for the agentic workforce.
topics.summary: Astrix and WideField secure non-human identities such as service accounts, API keys, and AI agents; Cisco's purchase makes identity the central control across its security platform.

sources.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions

weather: This Afternoon: Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 85F.
Tonight: Chance Showers And Thunderstorms then Partly Cloudy, low 67F.

games.recap: Eugenio Suárez hits 3-run homer in 9th as Reds rally for 9-7 win over Pirates

business_politics.title: Iran-U.S. Strikes Widen to Bahrain and Kuwait
business_politics.latest_developments: Iran extended its retaliation to Kuwait on June 28, threatened a complete halt to the peace talks if Washington keeps striking, and its foreign minister claimed sole Iranian control of the Strait of Hormuz, going beyond the second-day strikes and Bahrain drone assault reported June 27.
business_politics.summary: Iran hit Bahrain and Kuwait in response to renewed U.S. airstrikes inside Iran, straining the preliminary ceasefire, while Tehran asserted command of the Strait of Hormuz—the chokepoint for roughly a fifth of the world's oil—and NYK chief executive Takaya Soga warned that sea mines will hold shipping there to half of prewar levels for months.

sources.title: Iran Asserts Sole Control of Hormuz, Warns Challenges Will Bring More Violence
US and Iran exchange strikes as ceasefire falters
Mines will hold back Strait of Hormuz shipping for months, CEO warns

business_politics.title: BIS Warns AI Boom Risks a Lengthy Bust
business_politics.latest_developments: The Bank for International Settlements warned June 28 that AI 'exuberance' could end in a prolonged investment bust threatening the global economy, the same week the S&P 500 and Nasdaq fell in every session.
business_politics.summary: The Basel-based central bankers' bank cautioned that weak returns on AI spending could trigger a sharp pullback in funding for tech companies, a warning that landed alongside short-seller Carson Block's essay on a coming crash and analyses flagging globally stretched chipmaker valuations.

sources.title: AI 'exuberance' risks ending in lengthy investment bust, BIS warns
Stock Rally Collides With a New Slate of Worries

business.title: First U.S. Chrome Plant in Decades Lands in New Castle
business.latest_developments: AMG will open the first chrome production facility built in the United States in decades, in New Castle, the Post-Gazette reported June 28.
business.summary: The plant in New Castle, Lawrence County, will produce chrome used in airplanes, engines, and rockets, reviving domestic output of a metal long sourced abroad.

sources.title: Airplanes, engines, rockets: First chrome production facility in the U.S. in decades comes to New Castle

business.title: Pennsylvania May Tap Rainy Day Fund to Close Budget
business.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette reported June 28 that lawmakers may have to draw on Pennsylvania's rainy day reserves to balance this year's state budget.
business.summary: Harrisburg faces a shortfall large enough that legislators are weighing tapping the commonwealth's emergency savings, raising the prospect of a budget that leans on reserves rather than recurring revenue.

sources.title: Why lawmakers may need to tap Pa.'s rainy day reserves to balance this year's budget

business.title: Caliente Pizza Wins 10th World Title in Naples
business.latest_developments: Caliente Pizza & Drafthouse returned from the Caputo Cup in Naples, Italy, with its 10th world championship title, WPXI reported June 28.
business.summary: The Pittsburgh-area pizzeria competed against the world's top makers at the prestigious Caputo Cup in Naples, the birthplace of the pizza, adding another championship to a long run of wins.

sources.title: Caliente wins 10th World Championship title at international pizza-making competition

around_town.title: North Irwin Route 993 Bridge Finally Breaks Ground
around_town.latest_developments: TribLive reported June 28 that construction of the new Route 993 bridge will begin after more than a dozen years of planning, design, and fundraising.
around_town.summary: The new span carrying Route 993 links North Huntingdon to North Irwin in Westmoreland County, replacing a crossing whose replacement stalled through 12 years of delay.

sources.title: North Irwin Route 993 bridge construction set to begin after 12-year delay

around_town.title: Sheriff Warns of Jury Duty Phone Scam
around_town.latest_developments: Allegheny County Sheriff Kevin M. Kraus issued a warning June 28 about a phone scam threatening arrest over missed federal grand jury duty.
around_town.summary: Callers posing as authorities tell county residents they face arrest for skipping federal grand jury service, a ruse Sheriff Kraus says aims to frighten people into paying.

sources.title: Allegheny County sheriff warns of jury duty phone scam

around_town.title: Route 910 Paving Hits Another Stretch
around_town.latest_developments: WPXI reported June 28 that a paving project will affect another section of Route 910, Gibsonia Road, for months.
around_town.summary: Drivers on Route 910 in the northern Allegheny County suburbs face months of construction as crews pave a further stretch of the Gibsonia Road corridor.

sources.title: Paving project to start on another section of Route 910

events.title: Northside Music Festival Returns July 10-12
events.latest_developments: TribLive reported June 28 that the Northside Music Festival returns for a fourth year, running July 10 through 12, a Friday through Sunday.
events.summary: The Northside Music Festival fills the North Side's streets and businesses with a curated lineup of musical acts from Friday, July 10, through Sunday, July 12.

sources.title: Northside Music Festival set to return for 4th year

team_usa.title: USMNT Carries Deep Leadership Into Knockouts
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN wrote June 28 that the U.S. men's roster is full of leaders of varied temperaments, beyond captain Tim Ream, as the team enters the World Cup round of 32.
team_usa.summary: ESPN argued the United States' knockout-round success traces to a locker room rich in leadership of different types, with the co-hosts set to face Bosnia and Herzegovina after winning Group D.

sources.title: USMNT squad is full of leaders for World Cup knockouts

team_usa.title: U.S. Run Makes Sportsbooks Nervous
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN reported June 28 that the United States' momentum entering the knockout stage will have sportsbooks sweating more with each win.
team_usa.summary: France and Argentina top the World Cup title odds as the round of 32 opens, but the host United States' positive form has bookmakers bracing, since heavy public backing leaves them exposed every time the Americans advance.

sources.title: Sportsbooks sweat U.S. run entering knockouts

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: Notes From The Bubble, Volume 1
reading.summary: Zitron launches a running series chronicling what he sees as the inflating and increasingly precarious AI investment bubble, gathering ongoing evidence of overspending and weak returns across the tech industry.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Figma CEO Dylan Field About Design and AI
reading.summary: Field discusses building Figma and argues that AI works as a tailwind for the design company rather than a threat, expanding the market for the kind of work Figma's tools enable.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport criticizes AI firms for marketing their products through alarming claims about their own dangers, arguing the self-styled doom warnings function as hype rather than honest risk assessment.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
